title: Entity 455
wikitext:
{{User:ReDcosmic/Felix}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Entity 455: "Felix"}} {{Blurbackground|file=Felix1.png}}
Entity 455: Felix "His owners are still searching for him."

{{Entity Class|no=455|Dan=2|Hab=Majority|Int=3|Agg=2}}
{{Audio|file=Felix.mp3|align=center|volume=0.15|round=yes}}

'''DESCRIPTION'''
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
[[File:Felix1.png|300px|center|link=]]

'''Entity 455''', otherwise known as '''''Felix''''', is an entity that exists within a majority of levels inside the Backrooms, particularly in darker areas. It is a small, short-haired domestic tabby cat with soft, grey-brown fur patterned with narrow mackerel stripes and a white spot that extends up to the cat's throat. '''''Felix''''''s gender is unknown, although it is speculated to be a male cat, due to its behaviour akin to that of a male cat. It is usually found wandering in levels such as [[Level 11]], [[Level 999]], and rarely [[Level 224]], [[Level 4]], and other levels. It will occasionally sleep when it's too tired to wander around.
'''BEHAVIOR'''
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
[[File:Felix2.png|300px|center|link=]]

'''''Felix''''' usually wanders around in dark places looking for lower-tier entities and is typically friendly to people until provoked. When it encounters a wanderer, it usually stops for a few seconds to assess the situation. If it is a hostile human, it will either fight back or flee, but if it is harmless, it will remain in its current location. '''''Felix''''' can make regular rain noises, similar to those heard at [[Level 4]]. Felix can also no-clip, allowing him to move across levels at will. '''Felix''' has been seen to play with [[Entity 30|peekers]] when spotted, often chasing them due to their speed. When a victim is spotted, '''''Felix'''''' will take a low, ready stance before darting into the darkness to seize its target. While hunting, it unsheathes its sharp and long claws, scratching and inflicting significant damage to the prey. At the same time, it uses its mouth to bite and prevent the entity from escaping, allowing '''''Felix''''' to take down and eat the entity's corpse until only bones remain. In addition to hunting, it frequently consumes foods from the Backrooms, such as [[Object 1|almond water]] and leftover cat food. '''''Felix''''' is observed to follow wanderers who feed him, becoming dependent on them and may even bring food to the individual in return; however, these are usually dead and contaminated entities that are not for human consumption.
'''BIOLOGY'''
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
[[File:Felix3.png|300px|center|link=]]

As aforementioned, it is a small, short-haired domestic tabby cat with soft, grey-brown fur patterned with narrow mackerel stripes and a white spot that extends up to the throat. The cat's paws have "white socks" on them, while both back paws are totally white. The cat's face shows the classic tabby "M" marking on the forehead and a pale muzzle, alongside a slightly "aggravated" look. Additionally, there are darker lines that frame the eyes and extend from the outer corners to the cheeks. Its eyes are pale greenish-yellow, and the ears seem a little darker. '''Entity 455''' has a medium, well-proportioned build. The nose has warm, neutral tones, and the muzzle's pale fur contrasts with long, prominent whiskers.
'''DISCOVERY'''
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
[[File:Felix4.png|300px|center|link=]]

During an excursion in [[Level 79]], a group of [[M.E.G.]] explorers discovered '''''Felix''''' inside the snow in December 2012, damaged but alive. The M.E.G. took the cat to the vet to have its wounds treated before bringing it into their lab for inspection and behavior documentation. They provided Backrooms with the material, and the organization produced an article about the entity. A member chose to feed it, becoming the cat's new pseudo-owner.
'''ADDENDUM'''
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
Two sticky notes have been stuck onto '''''Felix''''''s body. When re-arranged, it reads as follows.

"Tribute to a Beloved Cat"
Today, my cat friend Felix died. Although he belonged to the neighbors and I had my own cat, he felt like my own. The neighbors didn't take good care of him and rarely took him to the vet, but we couldn't officially help because we didn't have the owner's permission or papers. Felix always came to us for comfort. He loved to play with my family and me. Even though my cat Flocke didn't get along with him, we still loved Felix. He was friendly, and we fed and cared for him like a small child. I always used to say that he had a nice dress when I was little. The neighbors also left the poor cat alone at home. We went to the neighbor's house to comfort and take care of him. One day, I was out in the garden when my cat, Flocke, made a strange sound. I looked toward the neighbor’s fence that separates our properties and saw Felix jump down onto our terrace. After the jump, he collapsed onto the terrace floor. I ran to him immediately to check for a pulse, but there was none. He was dead. I cried and wished the neighbors had taken better care of him. Sadness and anger came into me. I cried for hours. Eventually, I needed to give him a proper farewell. I placed him gently in a box with a note and buried him in my garden. I hope he goes to heaven. I miss him so much. I can’t write anymore now; my heart hurts too much. So long, Felix. You were a great friend. - Tom Schindler
All best to the owner of the cat.
'''RECOMMENDATIONS'''
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
'''Do...'''
> Treat it like a normal cat. <
> Feed it actual cat food; it can become clingy, but it can fend off some entities. <

'''Don't...'''
> Attack '''''Felix'''''. It might attack you instead, and it will be more painful than a regular Frontrooms cat. <
> Let it starve. <
> Touch the cat. It is sensitive and might attack you. <
